tasks.description: Runbook UI — 'Run as Checklist' button on section panel, runbook view (linear checklist with progress bar), runbook list page, sidebar nav link
acceptance_criteria: 'Run as Checklist' button visible on section detail panel — calls createRunbook API (workspace_id, section_id, name derived from section name) and navigates to runbook view

Runbook view page at /w/[workspaceId]/runbooks/[runbookId] — shows runbook name, status badge, progress bar (completed/total), linear checklist of runbook_steps with checkbox, step name, status, notes field
Clicking checkbox on a runbook step calls updateRunbookStep to toggle status (pending↔completed), sets completed_at
Runbook list page at /w/[workspaceId]/runbooks — lists all workspace runbooks with name, status badge, progress (e.g. '3/7'), section name, created date
Sidebar nav link for Runbooks (below Comments in nav order)
'runbooks' added to workspace-shell.tsx reserved paths array

patterns_to_follow: Comments page pattern: server page.tsx fetches data via Supabase server client, passes to client CommentsView component
TaskPanel pattern: checkbox list with inline editing, status toggles via API calls
Sidebar nav: lucide-react icon + label, link to /w/[workspaceId]/runbooks
workspace-shell.tsx line 47: add 'runbooks' to reserved paths array
Badge variants: default (blue), secondary (gray), destructive (red), warning (yellow) — use for runbook status
Progress bar: simple div with width% for completed/total ratio
Dark theme: bg-[var(--bg-primary)], text-white/90, borders via var(--border-subtle)
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — return apiFetch<T>() directly
Never sort prop arrays directly — use [...arr].sort()

tasks.special_instructions: The runbook API and types already exist from iteration 80 (FEAT-047 [1/3]). Client wrappers (fetchRunbooks, createRunbook, fetchRunbook, updateRunbook, deleteRunbook, fetchRunbookSteps, updateRunbookStep) are all in client.ts. Types (Runbook, RunbookStep, RunbookStatus, RunbookStepStatus) are in database.ts. This task is UI-only — no API or migration work needed. For the 'Run as Checklist' button: use createRunbook({ workspace_id, section_id, name: section.name + ' — ' + new Date().toLocaleDateString() }), then navigate to the new runbook's view page using router.push. For the runbook view: fetch runbook + runbook_steps, render as linear checklist. Steps should be ordered by position field. Status toggles: pending→completed sets completed_at to now, completed→pending clears completed_at. For navigation from section panel to runbook view, import next/navigation useRouter.

tasks.description: Add clickable navigation links from workspace comments page to source entities (steps, sections, stages, touchpoints)
acceptance_criteria: Each comment row in CommentsView shows entity name as a clickable link
Clicking the link navigates to /w/[workspaceId]/[tabId] for the correct tab containing the entity
If tab ID is not available in the comment data, link to the workspace root instead
Link styled consistently with dark theme (text-[var(--accent-blue)] hover:underline)

patterns_to_follow: CommentsView receives initialComments and entityNames as props from server page.tsx
Comments have commentable_type (step|section|stage|touchpoint) and commentable_id fields
To resolve tab_id: the server page.tsx needs to fetch steps/sections/stages/touchpoints to map entity IDs to their parent tab IDs
Use Next.js Link component for client-side navigation

tasks.special_instructions: The page.tsx server component already fetches comments. It needs to also build a mapping of entity_id → tab_id so CommentsView can construct navigation links. For steps: step.section_id → section.tab_id. For sections: section.tab_id directly. For stages/touchpoints: stage.tab_id / touchpoint.stage_id → stage.tab_id. Pass this mapping as a new prop (entityTabMap: Record<string, string>) to CommentsView. In CommentsView, wrap entity name in a Link to /w/{workspaceId}/{tabId}.

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed. Both tasks use existing API client wrappers and types from iteration 80.

testing_plan.regression_reason: Last regression was iteration 79 (40/40 PASS, 2 iterations ago). Risk score 2 (UI-only changes). Next regression due at iteration 87 (cadence floor).
